genai AI applications : Learn different agent design paradigmsMore agent (read: tool) options at your disposal to design genai solutions. Some current paradigms:
- Good ol' Prompt engineering
- RAG agent, and google Agentspace agent
- sub-topic : chunking RAG
- ReAct (Reasoning + Acting) Agents
- Multi model agent
- Deployment : Serverless AI agent and Managed AI Agent
- Crawl4AI - incorporate web scraping to provide context
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) Servers – consolidated tools for Agents
- Building & publishing to MCP registry
- OpenAI Function-Calling Framework
- Multi agent
- Libraries google ADK or lang chain
- DAG orchestrator n8n		- Quickstart the adk. Build a sample agent using these as a starter
- adk samples from the adk repo themselves
- adk made simple (youtube guide)



- Prompt engineering course , prompt engineering whitepaper
- Sample ReAct agent
- Sample agents, multi agent from google adk

... since we're in a hype train right now can't really tell which course and paradigm gonna stay after this.
Here's a wise word from a developer that's gone through similar phase : (reddit)

I approached it the same way as I approached JS frameworks and libraries during the JS dark ages. I just went for the one that was made sense to me and was easy to start. When doing angular I felt there was to much for me to get, then react came out and I felt a little less like I had to use every single thing in the cabinet, but I could focus on smaller parts of the library at first. Then came personal projects and then a job. For these AI frameworks I started doing basic things with it like scraping websites and embeddings to talk to the pdf. 		FALSE- Learn vertex ML evaluation cloud path to earn MLOps skills
RAG Pipeline EvaluationLearn how to eval joon agents
- google adk eval
- vertex eval service
TRUEcreated a notebook to demo eval with vertex ai service
https://github.com/joon-solutions/joon-agents/pull/5
